title: Filing suggestion UI
description: Frontend component showing daily note blocks with suggested filing destinations. User can accept (triggers MCP noteplan_edit_content to move block) or dismiss.
close_reason: Filing assistant UI: daily note selector sidebar, content block cards with kind badges/tags/links, suggestion targets with scores and File button (MCP-gated), skip/dismiss, new Filing tab in App.tsx

title: Filing suggestion engine
description: Match daily note content blocks to filing targets. Start with keyword/tag matching; optionally use MCP noteplan_embeddings for semantic matching if available.
close_reason: Filing suggestion engine with weighted scoring (wiki links, tag overlap, title keywords), 8 tests, Tauri command, frontend types

title: Filing target identification
description: Use folder structure + note titles to build a map of filing targets — existing project/domain/reference notes where blocks could be filed.
close_reason: Filing target builder with 6 tests, FilingTarget model, Tauri command, frontend types and API wrapper

title: Daily note content block extraction
description: Parse daily notes into discrete content blocks (paragraphs, task groups, headings). Each block is a candidate for filing into a project/domain note.
close_reason: Block extraction parser with 10 tests, Tauri command, frontend types and API wrapper

title: Add config flag for parsing backend
description: Settings UI toggle: Rust (fast, file-based) vs MCP (NotePlan API). Only if benchmarks show MCP is viable. Depends on MCP integration and benchmark beads.
notes: Benchmark results (2026-03-16): Rust full parse ~1200ms for ~1600 notes. MCP list-only ~230ms, MCP get ~1ms/note (~1.6s total). MCP retrieval is competitive on raw I/O but doesn't include structural parsing — Rust parser still wins for bulk scanning. Config toggle is low-value: keep Rust for scanning, MCP for targeted ops (writes, search, embeddings). Recommend deferring or closing as won't-do.
close_reason: Benchmark shows Rust parser wins for bulk scanning. MCP get is fast (~1ms/note) but still needs Rust parsing on top. Config toggle adds complexity for no user benefit.

title: Benchmark Rust file parsing vs MCP note retrieval
description: Compare scan times for ~1600 notes using current Rust parser vs MCP noteplan_get_notes. Measure latency, throughput, and memory. Results inform whether a config toggle is worth adding.
close_reason: Benchmark command compares Rust scan_noteplan_dir vs MCP list + sample get. Results shown via toast. Config toggle (bead 3iz) decision deferred to after real-world benchmarking.

title: MCP server integration layer
description: Add Rust MCP client that can spawn and communicate with @noteplanco/noteplan-mcp via JSON-RPC/stdio. Optional dependency — app works without it.
close_reason: MCP integration layer implemented: rmcp client, typed tool wrappers, Tauri commands, frontend API + UI toggle

title: Add version display to app (crate version + git short rev)
description: Display app version in the traditional macOS 'About' spot. Show both the Cargo crate version and git short SHA. Investigate Tauri v2 conventions for version display.
close_reason: Implemented: build.rs embeds git short rev, get_git_rev Tauri command, core:app:default capability, frontend fetch via getVersion()+getGitRev(), status tray display with v0.1.0 (rev) format

title: Rework ID Consistency Analyzer
description: Remove parent-prefix matching, add duplicate sequential ID check, bare 00 error, and JD-dotted migration flag

title: Restrict Cross-wired ID Analyzer to folders only
description: Skip notes with Sequential/DatePrefix/HubCode IDs, only check folder-to-folder hierarchy

title: Add hub code detection to Missing Hub Analyzer
description: Detect hubs by 00.{P,D,R}H filename in addition to section-heading heuristic

title: Add NoteIdKind enum to model layer
description: Add NoteIdKind enum (JdDotted, HubCode, Sequential, DatePrefix) to note.rs, add note_id_kind field to Note, and matching TS type

title: Add hub code detection to Hub Completeness Analyzer
description: Same hub detection change as missing hub task

title: Expand parser to recognize new ID formats
description: Add parse_note_id() to folder.rs with hub/date/sequential regexes, wire into parse_note()

title: Update ID system to support sequential/date-based note IDs
description: Parent epic: Decouple note IDs from folder IDs. Notes use independent sequential integers, dates, or hub codes instead of requiring parent-prefix matching.

title: Global task triage (Phase 4)
description: Use noteplan_paragraphs.search_global for structured task queries. Depends on MCP integration layer.

title: Semantic link suggestions (Phase 3)
description: Use noteplan_embeddings to find conceptually related but unlinked notes. Depends on MCP integration layer.

title: Fix actions on findings via MCP
description: Add Fix buttons to existing findings that can auto-remediate through MCP (move cross-wired notes, complete stale tasks, etc.). Depends on MCP integration layer.
close_reason: Fix actions: added FixAction model (label, tool, arguments) to Finding struct. StaleTask findings get 'Complete task' fix action via noteplan_paragraphs. Frontend shows hover-reveal Fix button on cards with fix_action, MCP-gated. Auto-dismisses on success.

title: NotePlan Spaces support
description: Use noteplan_folders.list_spaces to support multiple vaults.

title: Calendar cross-reference analyzer
description: Use noteplan_eventkit to find days with calendar events but no daily notes.
